message.content: Adversaries may gather victim identity information, specifically credentials, to facilitate further attacks. These credentials can be directly associated with the target organization or exploit users' tendency to reuse passwords across personal and business accounts. Common methods of credential gathering include phishing, compromising websites to collect authentication cookies, exploiting data leaks, and purchasing credentials from dark web markets. This information can be used for reconnaissance, establishing operational resources, or gaining initial access to victim environments. Notable groups like APT28, APT29, Chimera, LAPSUS$, Leviathan, and Magic Hound have employed these tactics. Mitigation strategies include enforcing strong, unique passwords, implementing multi-factor authentication, monitoring for suspicious account activity, educating users on phishing risks, and regularly updating and patching systems to prevent exploitation.
